name: Update Sign
desc: Player finishes editing a sign. Sends the block position and four lines of text as IChatComponents.

fields.name: lines
fields.desc: 4 lines of sign text as JSON ChatComponents

notes: Jigsaw 0.26 OPSign module cancels and resends C12 twice to crash the server's sign data structure on vanilla 1.8-1.8.5 servers (exploit patched in 1.8.8+).

implementation.overview: C12PacketUpdateSign sends the four lines of sign text when the player finishes editing a sign. Each line is an IChatComponent (JSON). Jigsaw 0.26 has an OPSign exploit module that sends C12 twice (cancel+resend in onPacketSent) to trigger a server crash on vanilla 1.8-1.8.5 servers. AutoSign modules (Gugustus) auto-fill signs with predefined text including color codes.

modules.name: OPSign Exploit (Jigsaw 0.26)
modules.purpose: Crash vanilla 1.8-1.8.5 servers by sending the C12PacketUpdateSign twice — cancelling the first and resending. This corrupts the server's sign text data structure and causes a NullPointerException that crashes the server thread.
modules.how_it_works: When C12PacketUpdateSign arrives, the module calls packet.cancel() to stop the original, then sendPacketFinal() to resend the SAME packet. The server receives it twice — once from vanilla send (which triggers processUpdateSign) and once from the module resend (which tries to process it again, but the tile entity state is already modified). The double processing on vanilla 1.8-1.8.5 causes an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException in TileEntitySign, crashing the server.
modules.vanilla_hook: Jigsaw onPacketSent() hook in NetworkManager.sendPacket() — OPSign cancels C12 and resends it. Server receive: NetHandlerPlayServer.processUpdateSign(C12PacketUpdateSign) updates the TileEntitySign.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: OPSign is not an anti-cheat concern — it's a server-crash exploit. The vulnerability was patched in 1.8.8+ with proper null checking in TileEntitySign. Modern servers (Spigot, Paper) are not affected as they have their own sign handling.

modules.name: AutoSign / ColorSign (Gugustus)
modules.purpose: Auto-fill sign text with predefined lines and inject § color codes for colored signs on servers that allow § characters.
modules.how_it_works: The module detects S36PacketSignEditorOpen (sign edit GUI opened). When the sign is submitted, it sends C12 with predefined lines containing § color codes.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Servers strip § color codes from sign text on Spigot/Paper. AutoSign is not detectable by anti-cheats.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: GuiEditSign sends C12 when 'Done' clicked. Jigsaw 0.26 hooks via Jigsaw.onPacketSent().

Server: NetHandlerPlayServer.processUpdateSign(C12PacketUpdateSign) validates position is a sign tile entity and updates it.
implementation.client_variations:
Jigsaw 0.26: OPSign exploits server crash via double C12 send. AutoSign fills sign text.
Gugustus: AutoSign sends colored text via C12.
Sigma 4.11: No specific C12 module.
